_comment: Fabric Real-Time Hub topology for Tales & Timber.
This file documents all data streams flowing through the tenant-wide
Real-Time Hub.  It is a reference map — the actual hub configuration
lives in the Fabric portal and is assembled from the Eventstream,
Event Hub, and Change Event Streaming resources provisioned by Terraform.
Use this topology to onboard new consumers, audit data lineage, and
plan capacity (throughput estimates assume 500 stores, $500 M/year).

streams.name: POS Transactions
streams.description: Point-of-sale transaction headers and line items from all 500 stores, captured via SQL Database Change Event Streaming.

tables.description: POS transaction headers — one row per sale
Line items for each transaction — one row per product per sale

consumers.name: Real-Time Dashboard
consumers.description: Store Operations Live dashboard — transactions per minute, revenue, top products

consumers.name: Anomaly Detection
consumers.description: Sales volume and revenue anomaly detection using series_decompose_anomalies

consumers.name: Data Activator Alerts
consumers.description: Reflex triggers for transaction spikes, unusual payment patterns, and fraud signals

throughput_estimate.notes: Base rate 3 txn/s × time-of-day multiplier (0.10–1.50) × weekend multiplier (1.3x). Each transaction has 2–3 line items on average.

schema_fields.name: Quantity
schema_fields.description: Units sold in this line item

schema_fields.name: PaymentMethod
schema_fields.description: Credit Card (45%), Debit Card (25%), Cash (15%), Digital Wallet (15%)

streams.name: Inventory Changes
streams.description: Stock-level changes from sales drawdowns and replenishment cycles across all 500 stores.

tables.description: Stock-level changes — decrements from sales, increments from replenishment

consumers.name: Stock Level Alerts
consumers.description: Inventory alert queries — out-of-stock, critical, low, below-reorder detection

consumers.name: Replenishment Pipeline
consumers.description: Data pipeline that triggers purchase orders when stock falls below reorder point

throughput_estimate.notes: Batch replenishment of 1–3 products per store every 30 seconds, plus stock decrements from each sale.

streams.name: IoT Sensor Telemetry
streams.description: Environmental and operational sensor data from IoT devices across all 500 stores — temperature, humidity, foot traffic, energy, door counters.

source_config.notes: IoT Simulator (Node.js) generates telemetry and pushes to Event Hub via AMQP

consumers.name: Store Environment Monitoring
consumers.description: IoT monitoring dashboard — temperature, humidity, sensor health across stores

consumers.name: Energy Optimization
consumers.description: Energy consumption trend analysis and HVAC efficiency tracking

consumers.name: Predictive Maintenance
consumers.description: Equipment health scoring and anomalous reading detection via Z-Score method

throughput_estimate.notes: 500 stores × 11 sensors/store × ~1 reading per sensor per 60s at full scale. Demo simulator runs at 10 events/s by default.

schema_fields.name: IsAnomaly
schema_fields.description: True if reading is flagged as anomalous (~2% rate)

streams.name: Customer Interactions
streams.description: Customer service interactions — calls, emails, chats, in-store visits, returns, complaints, and feedback.

tables.description: CRM interactions — phone, email, chat, in-store, returns, complaints, feedback

consumers.name: Sentiment Analysis
consumers.description: Real-time satisfaction score tracking and sentiment trend detection

consumers.name: Service Quality Tracking
consumers.description: Resolution rate monitoring, escalation alerts, and agent performance metrics

throughput_estimate.notes: Base rate 0.5 interactions/s. Seasonal peaks: Jan (1.5x), Nov (1.3x), Dec (1.8x).

schema_fields.name: InteractionType
schema_fields.description: phone_call, email, web_chat, in_store, return, complaint, feedback

schema_fields.name: SubjectCategory
schema_fields.description: billing, product_inquiry, return_request, technical_support, complaint, feedback, order_status

schema_fields.name: StoreId
schema_fields.description: Store identifier (nullable — not all interactions are store-specific)

schema_fields.name: SatisfactionScore
schema_fields.description: CSAT score 1–5 (nullable — only for resolved interactions)

streams.name: Web Clickstream
streams.description: Website and mobile app user activity — page views, clicks, searches, cart actions. Ingested from Lakehouse bronze in periodic micro-batches.

source_config.notes: Clickstream data lands in bronze Lakehouse via external pipeline, then micro-batched into Eventhouse every 5 minutes for near-real-time analysis.

consumers.name: Funnel Analysis
consumers.description: Conversion funnel tracking — landing → product view → add to cart → checkout → purchase

consumers.name: Personalization Engine
consumers.description: Real-time product recommendations based on browsing behavior and session context

throughput_estimate.notes: Batch-ingested every 5 minutes. Volume peaks during promotional events and holiday seasons.

schema_fields.name: EventType
schema_fields.description: page_view, click, search, add_to_cart, remove_from_cart, checkout, purchase

summary.retention_policy: 90 days for real-time tables, 30 days for clickstream
